similar_questions.title: Last Day Where You Can Still Cross
Number of Ways to Build Sturdy Brick Wall

discussion_posts.name: wanglu
discussion_posts.content:
I just want to share my thoughts here. 

If no bircks drop, then after all operations. The grid will be look like a pool with multi islands.
for example:
0010000100
0111001110
1111111111
after operations: [0,2], [2,4], [1,2], [0,7]
0000000000
0101001110
1111011111
so total 2 islands. 

Then add bricks back reversely.
[0,7]
0000000100
0101001110
1111011111
the right island attaches top, and its size is 9, which means 8 bricks drop in this operation.

[1,2]
0000000100
0111001110
1111011111
the left island does not reach the top, so no brick drops. 

[2,4]
0000000100
0111001110
1111111111
the left island connects to right island and acttaches top, and left island is original 7, which means 7 bricks drop in this operation.

[0,2]
0010000100
0111001110
1111111111
the island size is just enlarged by 1, which means no brick drops. 

Hope this helps:)

discussion_posts.name: cppshooter
discussion_posts.content: There\'s a [1D version of this problem](https://leetcode.com/problems/maximum-segment-sum-after-removals/) which can also be solved in a very similar way. If you\'re stuck on this problem try the 1D version first :)

discussion_posts.name: markalavin
discussion_posts.content: Can we assume that all initial bricks are stable?

discussion_posts.name: Dagar
discussion_posts.content: One of the facts that should be present in the problem statement is that all the bricks are stable to begin with.

discussion_posts.content: The problem with "hard" problems is understanding the problem is itself hard :-)

discussion_posts.name: panzerschrek
discussion_posts.content: This piece of shit is designed to be solved in reverse order - just remove all blocks in hits list and then add them back one by one.

discussion_posts.name: You_Know_Me
discussion_posts.content:
My O(R^2*C^2) solution is faster than 85% submissions and DSU solution with complexity O(R*C*alpha(R*C)) is only beating 69% of solutions. It's strange. 

Link to my O(R^2*C^2) solution. 
https://leetcode.com/problems/bricks-falling-when-hit/submissions/1613793241
